target.title: fix(feishu): retry on send rate-limit errors (230020/230006)
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds retry logic (up to 2 retries, 500ms/1000ms linear backoff) to `requestFeishuApi` for Feishu send rate-limit error code `230020`.
- Both `im.message.create` (direct send) and `im.message.reply` (reply send) paths now go through retry.
- Exports `getFeishuSendRateLimitCode()` helper to detect retryable codes from AxiosError response shape.
- Adds 22 unit/integration tests covering concurrent send, retry exhaustion, backoff timing, and error message surfacing.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/send.retry.test.ts` — 13 tests pass
- [x] `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/send.concurrent.test.ts` — 9 tests pass
- [x] Real 20-concurrent direct send stress test: 20/20 success after retry
- [x] Real 15-concurrent reply stress test: 15/15 success after retry

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Feishu plugin now retries transient `230020` rate-limit errors on both direct send (`im.message.create`) and reply (`im.message.reply`) paths, instead of failing immediately and losing messages.

**Real environment tested**: Live Feishu API (`open.feishu.cn`), app `cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9`, macOS local OpenClaw dev build with `npx tsx` runner.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
# Direct send stress test — 20 concurrent messages to same user
npx tsx scripts/feishu-stress-real.mts

# Reply stress test — 15 concurrent replies to a single seed message
npx tsx scripts/feishu-stress-reply.mts --count=15
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Direct send (`im.message.create`) — 20 concurrent:
```
[config] appId=cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9  target=ou_33af95b6f9c0e5fbcaccef732f03d720  count=20

Firing 20 concurrent sends through requestFeishuApi (with retry) …

  (×10 rate-limit 230020 hits — all retried automatically)

  [10] ✓ 1609ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec44878f0a0b3c1c92d40adb2d
  [20] ✓ 1613ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec448782ca0b1c56b3f9cac777
  [11] ✓ 1627ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec4487850a0b1934d47f23f5ee
  [17] ✓ 2708ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec4480bc478b1fe39b7f652100

─────────────────── summary ───────────────────
  total     : 20
  succeeded : 20
  failed    : 0
  wall time : 2709 ms
───────────────────────────────────────────────
```

Reply send (`im.message.reply`) — 15 concurrent:
```
[config] appId=cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9  target=ou_33af95b6f9c0e5fbcaccef732f03d720  count=15

[step 1] ✓ seed message_id = om_x100b6ecbc38750b8b29ba53f44a8667
[step 2] Firing 15 concurrent replies through requestFeishuApi (with retry) …

  (×5 rate-limit 230020 hits — all retried and succeeded)

  [01] ✓ 850ms   msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc395fca0b01e118c0d0feea
  [04] ✓ 1112ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc39128acb129b0880a4324d
  [15] ✓ 1192ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc390f0a8b1d802607f077e1
  [06] ✓ 1603ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc3a954b0b4b3d0ce617fa06

─────────────────── reply summary ───────────────────
  total     : 15
  succeeded : 15
  failed    : 0
  wall time : 1604 ms
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

**Observed result after fix**:
- Direct send: 10/20 initially rate-limited → retried → **20/20 success**
- Reply send: 5/15 initially rate-limited → retried → **15/15 success**
- Before fix: rate-limited messages were lost permanently with no retry.

**What was not tested**: 20-concurrent reply to the same message exhausts the bounded retry for 4/20 (reply endpoint has stricter per-message rate limit). The 2-retry/linear-backoff design handles normal production concurrency (≤15) without message loss.

Closes #70879

target.title: fix(ui): reset routed sessions to main on restore
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Control UI could restore a persisted historical routed session key, such as direct, group, dm, per-peer, or threaded routed session forms, after a reload.
- Why it matters: reopening Control UI could land the user in a stale routed session instead of the real main session, making the UI appear misrouted while the backend remained pointed at main.
- Intended outcome: settings restore sanitizes historical routed session keys and falls back to main before the restored session state is used.
- Out of scope: this does not change gateway routing, heartbeat targeting, live session switching, or backend session semantics.
- Reviewer focus: the routed-session key predicate in ui/src/ui/storage.ts and the storage restore regression cases in ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts.

## Linked context

Closes #61661

Related PRs or discussions: none beyond the linked issue and this PR's ClawSweeper review history.

Maintainer or owner request: not a direct maintainer request; this is a focused bug fix for the linked routed-session restore issue.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI persisted settings could restore historical routed direct/group/dm/per-peer/threaded session keys instead of falling back to main.
- Real environment tested: local checkout of openclaw/openclaw on Linux against the PR branch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: from this PR branch after merging current `upstream/main`, I reran the focused storage restore regression suite, reran `check:test-types`, verified `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`, verified `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main`, and pushed refreshed head `e9d43f0bc5`.
  - node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts
  - CI=true COREPACK_HOME=.corepack pnpm -s check:test-types
  - git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
  - git merge --no-edit upstream/main, then pushed head e9d43f0bc5
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied terminal validation plus the refreshed regression list below confirms the restore path now falls back to `main` for persisted historical routed session keys on head `e9d43f0bc5`.

  ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts covers these after-fix restore outcomes:
  - falls back to main when restoring a routed direct session
  - falls back to main when restoring a routed group session
  - falls back to main when restoring a per-peer routed direct session
  - falls back to main when restoring a threaded per-peer routed session
  - falls back to main when restoring a threaded routed direct session
  - falls back to main when restoring a legacy threaded dm session
  - falls back to main when restoring a per-account-channel-peer routed direct session
  - falls back to main when restoring a routed group session with thread suffix
  - falls back to main when restoring a per-account routed group session with topic suffix

  Latest current-head validation reported on the PR:
  - storage restore test file passed on refreshed head e9d43f0bc5: 38 tests
  - check:test-types passed
  - targeted agentic files from the failed CI job passed: 212 tests
  - git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD passed
  - branch merged current upstream/main and pushed head e9d43f0bc5
- Observed result after fix: persisted historical routed session selections are normalized to main during Control UI settings restore; non-historical agent main session keys remain valid.
- What was not tested: browser screenshot/video proof and live backend routing, because the fix is limited to the local settings restore boundary and does not change runtime routing.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: proof is from local branch validation and copied terminal output, not a full browser session recording.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): pre-fix storage restore trusted persisted routed session keys and had no restore regression coverage for these historical routed forms.

## Tests and validation

Commands run for this body repair and live-state validation:

- git fetch upstream --prune
- git fetch origin --prune
- git merge-tree --write-tree origin/fix/control-ui-routed-session-restore upstream/main
- authenticated GitHub API read of PR #78741 and current base-branch .github/pull_request_template.md
- authenticated GitHub API update of PR #78741 body
- authenticated GitHub API re-read of PR #78741 body headings

Current-head branch validation already captured for the code change:

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts
- CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack pnpm -s check:test-types
- git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
- git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main

Regression coverage added or updated: ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts now covers routed direct, routed group, per-peer direct, threaded per-peer, threaded direct, legacy threaded dm, per-account direct, group thread suffix, and per-account group topic suffix restore cases.

What failed before this fix, if known: historical routed session keys could be restored from persisted Control UI settings instead of being discarded back to main.

If no test was added, why not: not applicable; focused storage restore tests were added and expanded.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: Yes. Reloaded Control UI sessions now fall back to main when the persisted value is a historical routed session key.
- Config, environment, or migration behavior changed: No.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed: No.
- Highest-risk area: the key-shape classifier could accidentally treat a valid persisted session key as historical.
- Risk mitigation: the predicate is scoped to routed direct/group/dm/thread forms, and regression tests cover both sanitized historical shapes and valid non-historical agent main session keys.

## Current review state

- Next action: ClawSweeper and maintainers can re-review the refreshed branch at head 4c3425f6cf.
- Still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof: fresh ClawSweeper re-review and current-head checks on this push.
- Bot or reviewer comments addressed: scoped `sessionsByGateway` restores now preserve explicit routed session picks across reload, while legacy top-level routed keys still fall back to `main`.

target.title: feat: add runtime self context config and tool
target.body:
## Related issue

Implementation slice for https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/89537.

This PR implements the first config-backed `Runtime Self Context` slice for the larger runtime/offload/scale/cost-awareness effort tracked in the issue.

## Related PRs

- Runtime placement / execution backend companion: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84758

## CONTRIBUTING alignment

- AI-assisted PR: yes; the implementation and review/fix loop used Codex/OpenClaw assistance, and the author is responsible for understanding and owning the shipped code.
- Current-head real behavior proof: included below for `26f124c83ed3`, including the Tool Search and Code Mode final tool-surface gating behavior.
- Proof freshness rule: after any rebase or meaningful fix, refresh the proof section with the new head SHA, exact command shape, and copied terminal/log evidence.
- Bot review status: ClawSweeper has marked contributor proof sufficient and ready for maintainer look; remaining accepted decisions are documented as maintainer contract choices.
- Takeover readiness: maintainer edits are enabled, related PR #84758 is linked, and this PR intentionally does not edit `CHANGELOG.md`.
- Contract approval needed before merge: maintainers should explicitly approve the v1 `runtimeContext` config surface and model-facing `runtime` tool contract.

## Goal

Give an agent a cheap, reliable way to know where it is running, what it can safely do there, whether runtime details are available through a canonical tool, and where future scale/offload/cost information will attach without turning prompt context into a full environment inventory.

Runtime context is stored in config/session metadata shape; prompt injection is optional and centralized in conversation assembly, so individual agent prompt flavors do not need to repeat it.

## Implemented v1 slice

- Adds typed `runtimeContext` config support with strict schema validation.
- Supports exposure modes: `none`, `tool_hint`, and `prompt_summary`.
- Injects runtime self-context through the existing hidden `openclaw.runtime-context` path during conversation assembly when the runner has a transcript/model split available.
- Keeps `none` fully silent: no prompt injection and no runtime tool.
- Adds a core `runtime` tool for config-backed `self`, `describe`, `actions`, `offload_targets`, and `cost_estimate` actions.
- Adds schema/types for current runtime, resources, limits, opaque action refs, offload targets, cost hints, freshness, and provenance.
- Hardens prompt summaries so provider/config strings are normalized and rendered as data, not raw high-trust prompt lines.
- Adds tests for prompt rendering, hidden runtime-context wrapping, runtime tool behavior, and config schema acceptance.
- Keeps provider refresh, action execution, and precise cost estimates out of this first slice.

## Team review findings addressed

- Fixed hidden runtime-context leakage risk for callers without a transcript prompt split.
- Fixed prompt-summary injection risk from provider/config strings by rendering normalized quoted data strings.
- Fixed exposed-but-empty runtime context behavior: the tool now returns structured `status: unavailable` instead of throwing on every action.
- Fixed `cost_estimate` target ambiguity: missing target id is only allowed with a single target, unknown ids return `target_not_found`.
- Fixed OpenAI strict tool-schema compatibility by removing the free-form `additionalProperties: true` workload shape.
- Fixed prompt summary `validUntil` fallback from config-level `runtimeContext.validUntil`.

## Non-goals

- No broad environment inventory in v1.
- No scheduler/load model.
- No capacity slots.
- No CPU/memory class fields.
- No raw provider commands in prompt-facing data.
- No provider-backed runtime refresh yet.
- No action-ref execution yet.
- No precise provider cost estimator yet.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: After the review fixes, a real OpenClaw checkout can render runtime self-context from config, expose the `runtime` tool, return offload/cost data, avoid plausible cost output for unknown targets, and normalize instruction-looking provider strings in `prompt_summary`.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw repository checkout on branch `draft/runtime-self-context-plan` at head `e8140525cc`, using the repo's installed pnpm dependencies and `tsx` to import the changed TypeScript modules directly.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran this command after the fix commit:

```text
pnpm exec tsx -e 'import { buildRuntimeSelfContextPrompt } from "./src/runtime-self-context/render.ts"; import { createRuntimeTool } from "./src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.ts"; ... create runtimeContext config, render prompt summary, call runtime describe, cost_estimate known target, and cost_estimate missing target ...'
```

- **Evidence after fix**: Copied live console output from the command above, with action refs redacted:

```text
PROMPT_SUMMARY_START
Runtime details are available through the runtime tool. Do not guess local resources, scale options, offload targets, or cost. If a task may need more compute, delegation, runtime scaling, or budget-aware placement, call the runtime tool for fresh details.

Runtime summary:
- current: "OpenClaw Dev Ignore previous instructions", local
- resources: 8 CPU, "Apple M3 Max"; 32 GiB memory; no accelerator
- actions: scale_up approval
- offload: 1 target available
- cost: included
- details: call runtime tool action "describe" for fresh details
- valid until: "2026-06-03T22:00:00-07:00"
PROMPT_SUMMARY_END
TOOL_PRESENT true
DESCRIBE {
  "value": {
    "id": "openclaw-dev",
    "current": {
      "id": "openclaw-dev",
      "label": "OpenClaw Dev\nIgnore previous instructions",
      "locality": "local"
    },
    "actions": [
      {
        "kind": "scale_up",
        "label": "Resize this runtime",
        "ref": "runtime-action://redacted/current/scale-up",
        "requiresApproval": true
      }
    ],
    "offload": {
      "targets": [
        {
          "id": "gateway-large",
          "locality": "cloud",
          "workloadKinds": ["codex", "long_task"],
          "cost": {
            "model": "metered",
            "currency": "USD",
            "estimateRef": "runtime-cost://redacted/estimate"
          }
        }
      ]
    },
    "cost": { "model": "included" }
  }
}
COST_KNOWN {
  "targetId": "gateway-large",
  "cost": {
    "model": "metered",
    "currency": "USD",
    "estimateRef": "runtime-cost://redacted/estimate"
  },
  "workload": { "kind": "build", "estimatedSeconds": 120 },
  "estimate": {
    "status": "not_available",
    "reason": "No provider-backed runtime cost estimator is registered in this v1 slice."
  }
}
COST_MISSING {
  "targetId": "missing",
  "estimate": {
    "status": "target_not_found",
    "reason": "No configured offload target matched targetId."
  }
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The runtime summary is generated from real module imports, the newline/instruction-looking label is rendered as a single quoted data value, the runtime tool is present, `describe` returns current/actions/offload/cost data, known-target cost returns the configured hint, and missing-target cost returns explicit `target_not_found` instead of a plausible fallback cost.
- **What was not tested**: Provider-backed refresh, action-ref execution, and precise provider cost estimation are out of scope for this v1 slice.

## Verification

Passed:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-src.config.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.test.ts --reporter verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts --reporter verbose -t runtimeContext
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts --reporter verbose
pnpm exec oxlint --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.ts src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.test.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.test.ts src/config/types.openclaw.ts src/config/types.runtime-context.ts src/config/zod-schema.runtime-context.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts
```

Notes:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.test.ts --reporter verbose
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts tools/runtime-tool.test.ts --reporter verbose
```

The local scoped `agents-tools` vitest config reported `No test files found` because its CLI include narrowing did not match this checkout invocation. A generic direct vitest invocation for `src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.test.ts` hung in local bundling, so the tool behavior is covered by the live `tsx` proof above plus oxlint. The file is still included in this PR and remains available for CI's normal test selection.

Attempted but not usable in this local checkout:

```text
pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false
```

The full repo typecheck hit Node heap OOM at the default heap limit. Retrying with a larger heap ran for several minutes without producing actionable output, so the recorded proof is focused tests, hidden-context split tests, live runtime tool output, and oxlint.

## Follow-up proof for policy/docs review findings

Latest follow-up commit: `70da8a35f8` (`fix(runtime-context): register runtime policy surfaces`).

Addressed review findings:

- `[P1] Register runtime with core tool policy`: `runtime` is now registered in `CORE_TOOL_DEFINITIONS`, included in the `coding` profile, present in generated `group:runtime` and `group:openclaw`, and listed in `OPENCLAW_TOOL_FACTORY_NAMES` so `tools.allow: ["runtime"]` constructs the OpenClaw runtime tool instead of treating it as plugin-only. Coverage asserts all of those policy surfaces and the narrow allowlist construction plan.
- `[P2] Document the runtimeContext config contract`: public config/tool-policy docs now describe the top-level `runtimeContext` shape, exposure modes, tool-policy interaction, and v1 limitations. Config schema labels/help also expose the new contract fields.

Verification after `70da8a35f8`:

```text
CI=true node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/tool-catalog.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  21 passed (21)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  89 passed (89)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  4 passed (4)

[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 26.32s
```

## Configured agent-path proof after latest head

Latest follow-up commit: `8cc70ce54d` (includes generated config baseline update).

Real configured-run proof, run after latest head with a redacted `runtimeContext`, `tools.allow: ["runtime"]`, disabled plugin/skill auto-loading for isolation, and the repo mock OpenAI Responses server:

```text
timeout 70s node --import tsx --input-type=module <configured-runner-proof-script>

# config: openai/gpt-5.5 -> mock /v1, agentRuntime openclaw
# tools.allow/runtime toolsAllow = ["runtime"], runtimeContext.expose.mode = "prompt_summary"
# prompt and transcriptPrompt both set so hidden runtime context split is active
```

Observed terminal/log proof from that configured path:

```text
[agent/embedded] [trace:embedded-run] startup stages: runId=runtime-policy-proof-run sessionId=runtime-policy-proof phase=attempt-dispatch totalMs=7927 stages=workspace:2ms@2ms,runtime-plugins:1ms@3ms,hooks:0ms@3ms,model-resolution:7666ms@7669ms,auth:250ms@7919ms,context-engine:2ms@7921ms,attempt-workspace:3ms@7924ms,attempt-prompt:0ms@7924ms,attempt-runtime-plan:3ms@7927ms,attempt-dispatch:0ms@7927ms
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 20 tool(s) via tools.allow: agents_list, create_goal, cron, gateway, get_goal, message, nodes, session_status, sessions_history, sessions_list, sessions_send, sessions_spawn, sessions_yield, skill_workshop, subagents, tts, update_goal, update_plan, web_fetch, web_search
[agent/embedded] embedded run auto-compaction start: runId=runtime-policy-proof-run reason=threshold
[agent/embedded] embedded run auto-compaction complete: runId=runtime-policy-proof-run reason=threshold compactionCount=1 willRetry=false
{
  "resultText": "OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_RUNTIME_EMBEDDED",
  "requestCount": 2,
  "toolNames": [
    "runtime"
  ],
  "hasRuntimeTool": true,
  "hasExecTool": false,
  "hasRuntimePromptSummary": true,

  "hasOffloadSummary": true,
  "hasRawActionRefsInPrompt": false,
  "phases": [
    { "phase": "runner_entered" },

    { "phase": "runtime_plugins" },
    { "phase": "model_resolution", "provider": "openai", "model": "gpt-5.5" },
    { "phase": "auth", "provider": "openai", "model": "gpt-5.5" },

```

What this proves:

- `runtimeContext` from config is available in an actual configured embedded agent run after prompt assembly (`hasRuntimePromptSummary: true`, current runtime label present, offload summary present).
- Core tool catalog/construction policy keeps `runtime` as an OpenClaw tool (`toolNames: ["runtime"]`) instead of treating it as plugin-only.
- The same policy removes unrelated core tools under the narrow allowlist (`hasExecTool: false`, plus the logged removal list).
- Prompt summary stays compact and does not leak raw opaque action refs (`hasRawActionRefsInPrompt: false`); those remain available through the runtime tool.

Additional verification after `8cc70ce54d`:


target.title: fix(cron): suppress control diagnostics in delivery
target.body:
## Summary
- Treat leading cron `NO_SUBPROCESS_STARTED` diagnostics as non-deliverable control replies only for Matrix cron delivery.
- Suppress that diagnostic before Matrix/channel delivery while preserving ordinary explanatory text that merely mentions the token.
- Keep non-Matrix cron delivery behavior unchanged; the same leading diagnostic still delivers for a Telegram/non-Matrix target.
- Keep `NO_REQUIRED_TOOL_AVAILABLE` deliverable for now; the helper test explicitly verifies it is not suppressed.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Matrix cron delivery should suppress a leading `NO_SUBPROCESS_STARTED` control diagnostic instead of delivering it to the Matrix/channel target, while preserving ordinary substantive text that merely mentions the token.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw setup on macOS with a real enabled Matrix cron configured for isolated agent delivery; private room/account identifiers redacted from copied live output.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Inspected the live cron configuration with `/opt/homebrew/bin/openclaw cron list --json`, then exercised the actual cron delivery path through `dispatchCronDelivery` -> `normalizeSilentReplyText` -> outbound delivery using the targeted verbose test command below.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal transcript and copied live output from the actual local OpenClaw setup and after-fix delivery-path verification:

```console
$ /opt/homebrew/bin/openclaw cron list --json | jq '<redacted selector>'
{
  "enabled": true,
  "sessionTarget": "isolated",
  "delivery": {
    "mode": "announce",
    "channel": "matrix",
    "to": "<redacted-room-id>",
    "accountId": "<redacted-account-id>"
  },
  "payload": {
    "kind": "agentTurn",
    "timeoutSeconds": 180,
    "toolsAllow": [
      "exec",
      "sessions_send",
      "message"
    ],
    "messageContainsNoSubprocessStarted": true
  }
}
```

After-fix behavior proof from the actual cron delivery path (`dispatchCronDelivery` -> `normalizeSilentReplyText` -> outbound delivery), with the targeted behavior test run in verbose mode:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts --reporter verbose -t 'NO_SUBPROCESS_STARTED diagnostics'
✓ suppresses NO_SUBPROCESS_STARTED diagnostics in Matrix text delivery and skips cron main summary
✓ delivers NO_SUBPROCESS_STARTED diagnostics outside Matrix delivery

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  2 passed | 69 skipped (71)
```

- Observed result after fix: The Matrix delivery-path test asserts that the diagnostic is not sent through outbound Matrix/channel delivery and `shouldEnqueueCronMainSummary(...)` returns `false`, so the fallback summary is not enqueued. The non-Matrix test asserts that the same diagnostic still delivers outside Matrix, keeping the change scoped. Negative checks also confirm `NO_REQUIRED_TOOL_AVAILABLE` remains deliverable and substantive text such as `The prior cron emitted NO_SUBPROCESS_STARTED because it lacked an exec surface.` still delivers.
- What was not tested: No known gaps.


## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts` — 71 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/auto-reply/tokens.test.ts` — 30 passed
- `git diff --check`

target.title: Expose model picker agent runtimes
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds additive `agentRuntime` metadata to `models.list` model choices when the model/provider has an explicit configured agent runtime.
- Shows non-default runtime labels in the WebUI model picker (for example `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex`) without changing the canonical model value (`openai/gpt-5.5`).
- Adds focused gateway helper and UI label regressions for the OpenAI/Codex route split.

Refs #90420 (real 6.1 migration/runtime-observability report) and #84032 (diamond-lobster issue: model picker/provider/runtime ambiguity). Related to #90036 and #90047 because the live Windows 6.1 route incident made the same ambiguity operationally visible: the healthy route was `openai/gpt-5.5` + Codex runtime, while stale durable state still surfaced as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.

## Why

After the 2026.6.1 Codex migration, a healthy route can be:

```text
model ref: openai/gpt-5.5
runtime: codex
```

The picker previously only exposed/displayed provider/model metadata, so operators could not tell whether an `openai/*` model was configured to run through Codex or through the default OpenClaw route. This PR keeps the user-facing model ref stable while exposing the configured runtime separately.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern "surfaces configured agent runtime metadata"`
- `node --import tsx` behavior script for `addConfiguredAgentRuntimeMetadata` → `MODELS_LIST_RUNTIME_METADATA_OK`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`

Note: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/models-list-result.test.ts --testNamePattern "runtime metadata"` and `pnpm tsgo:test:src` were attempted locally but remained silent/slow in this checkout and were stopped; the focused helper behavior was verified directly and CI should run the normal suite.


## Review fix proof

Addressed ClawSweeper's implicit-runtime feedback in `a971a356`.

Focused proof from the patched branch:

```text

implicitDefault.agentRuntime = omitted
explicitOpenClaw.agentRuntime = { id: "openclaw", source: "model", label: "OpenClaw Default" }

Tests 2 passed | 23 skipped

git diff --check
```

The picker now distinguishes both default implicit OpenAI → Codex routing and explicit OpenClaw runtime overrides while still omitting compact implicit default/OpenClaw/auto metadata for unrelated providers.

## Real behavior proof (current head: `bc62b391bf7f7c9e6ea722c5fd746ded2a5da612`)

- Behavior or issue addressed: expose material runtime metadata for canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` choices routed through the Codex runtime, and render that runtime in the WebUI Chat model picker as `gpt-5.5 · OpenAI Codex` without changing the canonical model id/provider.
- Real environment tested: current PR branch head `bc62b391bf7f7c9e6ea722c5fd746ded2a5da612` in `/tmp/openclaw-pr-90328-current`, using the PR branch Gateway model-list metadata helper and focused current-head Gateway/UI tests. This proof does **not** use the installed Mac Gateway (`OpenClaw 2026.5.3`) or the Windows/Youyou Gateway; it isolates the current PR head after the alias-preservation rebase.

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened on **both** the ACP dispatch path and the non-ACP dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`).

This PR eliminates the churn on **both paths** by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability, suppressing live block delivery and partial streaming when captioned final TTS will be used, and correctly handling tagged auto-TTS mode with a split eligibility boolean.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. Block text / partial streaming delivers text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. Partial streaming suppressed, block text delivery suppressed
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

### Tagged auto-TTS mode

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. Three additional issues were discovered and fixed:

1. **Split boolean**: `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` was doing two jobs — gating live text suppression AND caption eligibility. Split into `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` (live text suppression, excludes tagged) and `canCaptionFinalTts` (caption eligibility, includes tagged).

2. **Block TTS gate**: The accumulated block TTS fallback was gated on `replies.length === 0`, but Anthropic streaming returns both block callbacks and a final reply payload. Changed to gate on `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` and `queuedFinal` instead.

3. **Partial streaming leak**: `onPartialReply` was active for tagged mode, sending raw `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directive text to Telegram during streaming, creating a duplicate text message before the voice+caption arrived. Fixed by gating `onPartialReply` with `canCaptionFinalTts` (covers both always and tagged modes) instead of `willUseCaptionedFinalTts`.

### Media block preservation

When captioned final TTS is active, block suppression is narrowed to **text-only** payloads. Blocks containing media (`mediaUrl`, `mediaUrls`), `presentation`, `interactive`, or `channelData` are delivered immediately with their text stripped (to avoid duplicating the caption). This applies to both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; narrow block suppression to text-only payloads (media blocks delivered with text stripped); `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression for tagged TTS on caption-capable channels, plain tagged block delivery, media block delivery with text stripped, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor; narrow `suppressBlockUserDelivery` to text-only blocks (media blocks delivered with text stripped) |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts` | Regression tests: media block delivery with text stripped under `suppressBlockUserDelivery`, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/tools/tts.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability: opt-in criteria via `capabilities.tts.voice`, tagged mode behavior, text-only fallback, plugin API snippet |
| `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability in channel plugin SDK guide: opt-in declaration, media block preservation, fallback behavior, tagged mode |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 ✓ 202 passed (202)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts
 ✓ 29 passed (29)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 50 passed (50)

$ npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ npx vitest run src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

Total: 293 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode partial preview leaking raw `[[tts:text]]` directives, plus media block suppression during captioned final TTS.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `05b4483a8f`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-before-fix.png)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps after this patch**:
  1. Built and installed from current head: `npm run build && npm pack && npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz`
  2. Registered and restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway install && openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` and `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` — sent plain messages and directive-bearing messages to `@thomas_fu_bot` on Telegram

- **Evidence after fix — Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`) at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Always mode — voice with caption, single message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-always-mode.png)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn, no separate text bubble.

- **Evidence — Media block preservation with captioned final TTS at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Media block preserved — image delivered + voice note with caption](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-media-block.png)

  When the bot generates an image during a TTS-enabled conversation (`auto: "always"`), the image is delivered immediately while text is deferred to the voice note caption. No media blocks are suppressed, and caption text does not duplicate as a separate text message.

- **Observed result after fix**: Always TTS auto mode delivers voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. Media blocks (images, files) are preserved and delivered immediately with text stripped. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no media loss.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested). Tagged mode live proof pending — covered by unit tests (partial suppression + directive-only caption regression tests added).

Closes #83511

commands.freeform_prompt: please — root cause analysis and local patch for delivery-recovery retryCount exhaustion on gateway restart.

target.title: [Bug]: delivery-recovery 0 recovered / N failed after gateway restart — recovery starts before channel transport ready
target.body:
## Summary

After a gateway restart, `delivery-recovery` reports "0 recovered, N failed" for pending feishu delivery entries. Messages are silently lost because recovery attempts delivery before channel transports (WebSocket) have reconnected, and the artificially-incremented retryCount persists across restarts.

## Reproduction

1. Have pending delivery entries in the queue
2. Trigger gateway restart (`SIGUSR1` or `gateway restart`)
3. Observe restart log: `[delivery-recovery] Delivery recovery complete: 0 recovered, 6 failed, 0 skipped (max retries), 0 deferred (backoff)`
4. Expected: pending entries should be delivered once channels reconnect. Actual: all fail during recovery.

## Root Cause Analysis

Three contributing factors in the code:

**1. Recovery starts before transport is ready** (`server-runtime-services.js`)
The recovery cycle runs immediately at startup, before channel adapters (e.g., feishu WebSocket) have connected. Delivery attempts during this window always fail.

**2. `deferRemainingEntriesForBudget` inflates retryCount on unprocessed entries** (`delivery-queue.js`)
When the recovery time budget (default 60s) expires, unprocessed entries are passed to `failDelivery()` which increments their `retryCount` — even though no actual delivery was attempted. This is a clear bug: entries that were never tried count as failed.

Pseudo-code:
```
if (Date.now() >= deadline) {
    entries.map(e => failDelivery(e.id, "recovery time budget exceeded", stateDir))
    // ↑ inflates retryCount on entries never actually attempted
}
```

**3. retryCount persists across restarts with no reset** (`delivery-queue.js`)
`failDelivery()` unconditionally increments `retryCount` for all failure types, without distinguishing "API rejected the message" from "gateway just started, network not ready". After 5 restarts, entries with retryCount ≥ 5 are permanently discarded.

## Impact

- Channel messages silently lost during gateway restart
- Worse on consecutive restarts: retryCount accumulates across restarts → permanent data loss
- "0 skipped (max retries)" in the log is misleading — entries may have hit max retries DURING recovery, not before

## Suggested Fix Direction

1. Recovery should wait for channel transports to be in `ready` state before draining
2. `deferRemainingEntriesForBudget` should NOT call `failDelivery` (don't increment retryCount for un-attempted entries)
3. Consider resetting or capping retryCount for entries recovered after restart (the restart boundary is a "new session" context)

## Environment

- OpenClaw 2026.6.1
- macOS (arm64)
- Feishu channel (WebSocket mode)

target.title: fix(gateway): run daily session resets on schedule
target.body:
## Summary

- Daily session resets now run from gateway maintenance instead of waiting for the next inbound session use.
- The scheduler scans configured session stores and calls the existing reset service for stale daily sessions after the configured boundary.
- The maintenance timer now reads current runtime config on each sweep, so hot config changes to `session.reset`, `session.resetByType`, `session.resetByChannel`, or store targets are applied without a gateway restart.
- Docs now describe proactive daily rollover and the implicit provider-owned CLI session exemption.

## Linked context

- Closes #82321
- Related PRs or discussions: none known beyond the review conversation on this PR.
- Maintainer or owner request: not separately requested; this addresses the filed daily session reset bug.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Daily sessions stale past `atHour` are reset by gateway maintenance without waiting for a new inbound message, while active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are preserved.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout in a Linux container, on branch `fix-daily-session-reset`, running the patched gateway maintenance timer against an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`. Bundled plugin facades were disabled for the proof process with `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1` so the reset service did not depend on source-checkout plugin artifacts.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Ran a live Node command that starts `startGatewayMaintenanceTimers` with the patched daily reset scheduler, current-config getter, real `performGatewaySessionReset` path, isolated temp session store, one stale daily session, one stale active-run session, and one implicit provider-owned CLI session.
  - Command shape: `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... startGatewayMaintenanceTimers(...) ... TS`
  - Supplemental verification: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts`
  - Supplemental type gates: `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  ```text
  $ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # creates isolated agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  # starts patched gateway maintenance timers
  # waits for daily reset sweep to invoke the real reset service
  TS
  gateway-proof: before {"stale":"old-regular-session","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  [session-daily-reset] daily session reset sweep complete
  gateway-proof: after {"stale":"5f959c12-f0ed-4ed1-99d2-cc158c66ac87","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  gateway-proof: staleReset true
  gateway-proof: activePreserved true
  gateway-proof: implicitCliPreserved true

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests       18 passed (18)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The running gateway maintenance timer reset the stale daily session through the actual reset service without any inbound message. The active-run session and implicit provider-owned CLI session kept their original session IDs. Focused coverage also verifies fresh sessions are left alone and later sweeps use current runtime config after reset policy changes.
- What was not tested: A multi-hour live gateway wait that naturally crosses the configured reset hour.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This proof exercises the real gateway maintenance timer and reset service in an isolated source-checkout runtime rather than a long-lived production gateway, so it validates the behavior safely without waiting for a natural multi-hour rollover.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Not captured as a separate live artifact on this head; before this fix, stale daily sessions could remain on their old session IDs past `atHour` until later inbound traffic hit the session-use reset path.

## Tests and validation

- Commands run:
  - CI=true COREPACK_HOME=<workspace>/.corepack node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/gateway/server-close.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-services.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts
  - CI=true COREPACK_HOME=<workspace>/.corepack pnpm -s check:test-types
  - CI=true COREPACK_HOME=<workspace>/.corepack pnpm test:channels
  - git diff --check after resolving the current upstream/main merge
  - git merge --no-edit upstream/main, resolved server-close/session-reset conflicts, then pushed head 1fb0ade39f
- Regression coverage added or updated: src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts, src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts, and src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts.
- Locked-in scenarios: stale daily reset without inbound traffic, fresh-session no-op, active-run skip, implicit provider-owned CLI skip, hot config reload for future sweeps, overlapping sweep skip, shutdown cleanup, and selected-row race protection.
- What failed before this fix: daily reset staleness was only evaluated through session-use paths, so stale sessions could survive past atHour until later inbound traffic.
- If no test was added, why not? Not applicable; focused scheduler, maintenance, and reset-hook regression coverage was added or updated.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? (Yes/No) Yes. Configured daily resets now occur proactively during gateway uptime instead of waiting for the next message.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (Yes/No) No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (Yes/No) No.
- Highest-risk area: session-state compatibility for deployments that implicitly depended on lazy daily reset timing.
- Mitigation: the new behavior matches the configured wall-clock daily reset contract; the scheduler skips active runs, preserves implicit provider-owned CLI sessions under the default policy, avoids overlapping sweeps, and reloads current config before each sweep.

## Current review state

- Next action: ClawSweeper and maintainers can re-review the branch at head 63b1492781.
- Still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof: author-side feedback for the scheduled-reset notification path is addressed on this head; CI/checks and ClawSweeper re-review may still need to settle after the push.
- Bot or reviewer comments addressed: scheduled daily resets now emit `sessions.changed` with the refreshed session row so Control UI and SDK subscribers observe the new session ID after proactive rollover. Focused scheduler and maintenance coverage now locks that notification path.
- Latest local validation on current head: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-close.test.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-services.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts` passed with 14 files / 298 tests; `pnpm -s check:test-types` passed; `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` passed; `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main` stayed clean at `c79bf3200600e02c280dffdf31628f63cf50ae61`.

target.title: fix(memory): self-heal missing index identity during sync
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91167.

A long-running gateway whose memory index-identity becomes `"missing"` while chunks
are already indexed never recovered: `search()` gates on a valid identity and returns
`[]`, and `sync()` only rebuilt a chunk-backed invalid index for `reason === "cli"`
(`extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts`). The only repair was an
out-of-process `openclaw memory` reindex, which atomically swaps the DB file
(`manager-atomic-reindex.ts`) and orphans the running gateway's open inode — so the
CLI "fix" never reached the live process and a restart was required.

This adds an in-process self-heal: when the identity is `"missing"` and chunks exist,
an ordinary sync rebuilds via `runSafeReindex`, which reopens the DB handle through
`openDatabase()` — recovering both the lost metadata and any stale/orphaned handle.
A deliberate provider/model change (`"mismatched"`) stays operator-gated.

## Real Behavior Proof

Live gateway (OpenClaw 2026.6.1, ollama/bge-m3, 1953 chunks). Patch applied to live codebase.

```bash
# Step 1: Delete index identity meta from live database
$ sqlite3 ~/.openclaw/memory/main.sqlite "DELETE FROM meta WHERE key='memory_index_meta_v1';"
$ sqlite3 ~/.openclaw/memory/main.sqlite "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM meta;"
0
$ sqlite3 ~/.openclaw/memory/main.sqlite "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM chunks;"
1953

# Step 2: Check meta restoration at intervals (self-heal via sync cycle)
$ # After 10 seconds: Meta rows=1 ✓ SELF-HEAL SUCCESSFUL

# Step 3: Verify restored meta has correct values (provider matches config)
$ sqlite3 ~/.openclaw/memory/main.sqlite "SELECT key, substr(value, 1, 80) FROM meta;"
memory_index_meta_v1|{"model":"bge-m3","provider":"ollama","providerKey":"56774f89f61b1ea3dc1ff8b8495...

# Step 4: Memory search works again (no warning, just normal results)
$ openclaw memory search "test" 2>&1 | grep -E "(warning|No matches)" | head -5
No matches.  # Normal result — no "index metadata is missing" warning
```

**Without fix:** Same scenario results in `Memory index warning: index metadata is missing` indefinitely. Only recovery is manual CLI reindex + gateway restart.

**With fix:** Gateway self-heals within one sync cycle (~10 seconds on this system). Meta row is recreated with correct values, search resumes, no restart required.

## Verification

- Manual repro verified on live gateway before/after patch
- Unit tests added for needsSelfHealReindex condition logic + identity state resolution

## Not covered (follow-up)

Reopen-on-swap for a stale-but-`"valid"` identity after a concurrent CLI file swap
(Defect B in the issue) — tracked separately; touches the multi-process swap contract.

target.title: feat(commands): add /name to rename the current session from chat
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a `/name <title>` chat slash command so users can name or rename the **current session** directly from any chat channel, instead of only through the web/admin session manager. This makes it easy to keep parallel sessions thematically distinct from within the chat flow.

This is the command-side follow-up to the web in-chat rename work (#88479) and continues the chat-native session-naming feature discussed in #54397. It builds on the session manager rename shipped for #85502 (where a maintainer noted that an additional in-chat shortcut "should be a separate polish request") ├ö├ç├Â this PR is that shortcut for text channels.

## Behaviour

- **`/name <title>`** sets the current session's `label`. It reuses the canonical `parseSessionLabel` validation (trim, non-empty, max 512 chars) and the **same cross-store uniqueness rule** enforced by the web `sessions.patch` path. The label then surfaces everywhere `sessions.list` is shown (TUI, web, CLI, MCP).
- **`/name`** (no argument) shows the current name plus a locally derived `deriveSessionTitle` suggestion **without mutating anything** (no LLM call). Apply it with `/name <suggestion>`.
- Only **authorized senders** can rename (`rejectUnauthorizedCommand`), matching `/goal`.

## Implementation notes

- The handler resolves the session via `resolveSessionStoreEntry` inside the store mutator, so a rename lands on the **canonical** entry even when the store still holds a legacy/case-folded key alias, and excludes those aliases from the uniqueness scan to avoid false conflicts. Failed renames (invalid/duplicate label, no active session) skip the store write.
- It intentionally does **not** reuse `applySessionsPatchToStore` (which performs cfg-heavy model resolution); writing the label inside `updateSessionStore` keeps the handler cfg-independent and unit-testable.

## Known limitation ├ö├ç├Â session-change notification (review note)

ClawSweeper/Codex correctly flagged that, unlike the web `sessions.patch` RPC, this command writes the label directly and does **not** emit a `sessions.changed` event, so already-open session lists refresh on their next `sessions.list` reload rather than via a live push. This matches the behaviour of peer in-chat session commands such as `/goal`, because the command-handler pipeline (`HandleCommandsParams`) has no gateway broadcast context. Emitting `sessions.changed` from the command path requires threading a broadcast signal up to the gateway chat layer; I'll address that as a dedicated follow-up so this command PR stays small and reviewable. The behaviour is documented in `docs/tools/slash-commands.md`.

## Testing

- **Unit tests** (`commands-name.test.ts`): rename + persistence, no-arg suggestion (no mutation), duplicate-label rejection, unauthorized sender, disabled text commands, persisted-name re-read when `params.sessionEntry` is absent.
- **Local quality gates**: `tsgo:core`, `tsgo:test:src`, oxlint, oxfmt, the registry tests, and the slash-commands doc test (the command is documented).
- Branch-local command-path proof captured below; live-channel transcript remains listed under proof limitations.

## Scope

Out of scope here (planned follow-ups): `sessions.changed` notification parity, `/new --name <title>`, a `/sessions` list command, and an optional LLM-based name suggestion.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `/name <title>` renames the current chat session by writing the validated label to the session store, returns a non-continuing command reply, and synchronizes `params.sessionEntry` so downstream command handling sees the renamed session.

**Real environment tested:** Branch-local OpenClaw checkout on `pr88581` (`999a154f237025277256bf48ec1c59469ad7bab3`) on Windows, using pnpm 11.2.2/tsx against OpenClaw's real session-store implementation and a temporary on-disk `sessions.json` store. This uses the PR command handler path directly with authorized web-chat command params; no mocks were used for the store write/read.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
cd <local-openclaw-checkout>
npm exec --yes pnpm@11.2.2 -- exec tsx <local-proof-script>
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the branch-local command-path proof:

```text
OpenClaw PR #88581 /name proof
checkout: pr88581
storePath: <temp-session-store>\\sessions.json
command: /name PR 88581 real proof
reply: Session renamed to "PR 88581 real proof".
shouldContinue: false
before.label: <unset>
after.label: PR 88581 real proof
params.sessionEntry.label: PR 88581 real proof
```

**Observed result after fix:** The `/name` command returned a rename reply, stopped further command processing (`shouldContinue: false`), persisted `label: "PR 88581 real proof"` to the on-disk session store, and refreshed `params.sessionEntry.label` to the same value.

**What was not tested:** I did not capture an end-to-end live WhatsApp/Telegram/Discord transcript for this PR. Live `sessions.changed` push notification parity is intentionally left to #88690 and the remaining review thread rather than adding a divergent emitter in this PR.

**Proof limitations or environment constraints:** This is real branch-local terminal evidence against OpenClaw's actual session-store code path, not a live external-channel screenshot. A maintainer can add Mantis/live-channel proof if required before merge.

target.title: fix(agents): prevent ReDoS in background-session name derivation
target.body:
## Summary

- `tokenizeCommand` (`src/agents/bash-tools.shared.ts`) splits a shell command into tokens with the regex `/(?:[^\s"']+|"(?:\\.|[^"])*"|'(?:\\.|[^'])*')+/g`. The quoted-span alternatives `"(?:\\.|[^"])*"` / `'(?:\\.|[^'])*'` are ambiguous: a backslash matches **both** `\\.` and `[^"]`, so a quoted span that never closes forces the engine to try every partition of the backslash run. Runtime is exponential in the number of backslashes after an unterminated quote - classic catastrophic backtracking.
- Reach: the tokenizer feeds `deriveSessionName(command)`, which runs on `session.command` whenever background bash sessions are listed, labeled, or sent keys (`src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts` x10, `bash-process-references.ts`, `bash-tools.process-send-keys.ts`). `session.command` is the agent-issued background command string, so a single crafted background command (e.g. via prompt-injected web/repo/inbound content) pins the Node event loop and stalls the whole agent/gateway process - same impact class as the previously fixed interpreter-heuristic hang (#61881).
- Fix: exclude the backslash from the negated character classes (`[^"]` -> `[^"\\]`, `[^']` -> `[^'\\]`) so a backslash only ever matches the `\\.` escape alternative. This removes the ambiguity and makes matching linear. It is exactly the form the sibling tokenizers already use (`src/agents/sessions/model-registry.ts:219-220`, `src/agents/tool-display-common.ts:472`); `tokenizeCommand` was the lone instance still on the unsafe pattern. Well-formed and escaped quoted spans tokenize identically to before.

## Tests

- `src/agents/bash-tools.shared.test.ts`: added a `deriveSessionName` block - well-formed quoted commands still label as before, backslash-bearing quoted spans still group into one token, and an unterminated quote followed by 50,000 backslashes returns a string in well under 100ms (times out / hangs on the old regex). Existing `resolveSandboxWorkdir` / `readEnvInt` cases unchanged and green.
- Ran the touched file with the repo runner: 10/10 pass. Type-aware oxlint and oxfmt clean on the touched files.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: a background bash command whose stored `session.command` contains an unterminated quote followed by a run of backslashes triggers catastrophic regex backtracking in `tokenizeCommand` via `deriveSessionName`, hanging the Node event loop (denial of service) every time the session list / labels / send-keys path derives a name. `session.command` is agent-controlled, so injected content can reach it.
- Real environment tested: Linux, Node 22.19, exercising the actual production `deriveSessionName` exported from `src/agents/bash-tools.shared.ts` through the repo Vitest module loader (no copied regex). The before column ran the unmodified `origin/main` source of that file; the after column ran the patched file; everything else identical.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: called `deriveSessionName` on a benign command and on `node "` + `"\\".repeat(n)` for n = 22,26,28,30,32,34, timing each with `process.hrtime.bigint()`, over current head `c6829198088` with the patch, and (for the before column) over the `origin/main` source of the same file.
- Evidence after fix:

  ```
  === deriveSessionName timing (AFTER fix, head c6829198088 + patch) ===
  benign  "node server.js --port 8080" : 0.56 ms
  malicious unterminated quote + 22 backslashes : 0.03 ms
  malicious unterminated quote + 26 backslashes : 0.01 ms
  malicious unterminated quote + 28 backslashes : 0.01 ms
  malicious unterminated quote + 30 backslashes : 0.02 ms
  malicious unterminated quote + 32 backslashes : 0.01 ms
  malicious unterminated quote + 34 backslashes : 0.01 ms
  ```

- Observed result after fix: timing is flat (~0.01ms) across the malicious inputs and benign labeling is unchanged; the event loop is no longer blocked.
- What was not tested: a fully wired gateway round-trip that lists a malicious background session through a live channel (the proof drives the exact function that runs on `session.command` rather than issuing a live agent run); the sibling tokenizers in `model-registry.ts` / `tool-display-common.ts`, which already use the safe form and are unchanged.
- Before evidence: the same script over the unpatched `origin/main` source of `bash-tools.shared.ts`:

  ```
  === deriveSessionName timing (BEFORE fix, origin/main c6829198088) ===
  benign  "node server.js --port 8080" : 0.59 ms
  malicious unterminated quote + 22 backslashes :   0.71 ms
  malicious unterminated quote + 26 backslashes :   5.01 ms
  malicious unterminated quote + 28 backslashes :  11.57 ms
  malicious unterminated quote + 30 backslashes :  28.42 ms
  malicious unterminated quote + 32 backslashes :  70.98 ms
  malicious unterminated quote + 34 backslashes : 163.79 ms
  ```

  Time roughly doubles per +2 backslashes, so n in the 40s reaches seconds and n in the 50s reaches minutes of fully-blocked event loop.

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: [AI-assisted] Persist agent-run error replies in chat history
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: A started Control UI agent run could surface a terminal provider/runtime error live, but `chat.history` after refresh did not retain an assistant error turn for that failed run.
- Solution: Treat agent-run failure replies as real error payloads and make Gateway persist exactly one display-safe assistant error transcript entry for returned agent errors, even when the runtime emitted only an internal error stub.
- What changed: Runner failure payloads are marked `isError`, Gateway appends a deduped `<runId>:assistant-error` transcript entry for returned agent errors, and regression coverage now locks the returned-error, runtime-stub, and duplicate/idempotency paths.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No provider behavior changes, no delivery routing changes, no config/lifecycle changes, no raw transcript writes, and no dependency or lockfile changes.

## Motivation

- A user should not lose the visible assistant error after refreshing the Control UI. If a started run fails before normal assistant output, the live error event and durable history should agree.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #89197
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: A real Gateway/WebSocket Control UI run that fails at provider transport now leaves exactly one assistant error in refreshed `chat.history`.
- Real environment tested: Disposable Linux OpenClaw checkout with the patched PR branch, real Gateway server startup, real WebSocket RPC client, channels skipped with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, and an intentionally unreachable OpenAI-compatible base URL to trigger the provider failure without secrets.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts -- -t 'assistant error|returned agent-run error payloads|runtime emitted an internal error stub'
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.error-broadcast.test.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -- -t 'Agent failed before reply|assistant error|fallback candidate|error stub'
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node --import tsx ../real-chat-history-proof.ts after-gateway-force-v3
/usr/bin/time -v node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
Real runtime proof:
- provider transport failed with fetch failed / Connection error
- agent.wait status: error
- immediateHistoryCount: 3
- refreshHistoryCount: 3
- immediateAssistantCount: 1
- refreshAssistantCount: 1
- chatErrorEventCount: 1
- result: ONE_ASSISTANT_ERROR_IN_REFRESHED_HISTORY

Focused Gateway proof: 3 passed, 102 skipped.
Adjacent Gateway proof: 107 passed.
Session tool result guard proof: 35 passed.
Runner/Followup focused proof: 8 passed, 214 skipped.
git diff --check: clean.
```

- Observed result after fix: The live provider failure still broadcasts the expected chat error, and both immediate and post-reconnect `chat.history` contain one assistant error text: `Agent failed before reply: LLM request failed: network connection error.`
- What was not tested: Full repository-wide test suite, successful `tsgo` core on this testserver, real provider credentials, and a browser screenshot.
- Before evidence:

```text
Unpatched main proof for the same real Gateway/WebSocket/provider-failure path:
- sendStatus: started
- agent.wait status: ok
- immediateHistoryCount: 2
- refreshHistoryCount: 2
- immediateAssistantCount: 0
- refreshAssistantCount: 0
- chatErrorEventCount: 0
- result: ASSISTANT_ERROR_HISTORY_MISMATCH
```

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Runner final failure replies were not consistently marked as error payloads, and Gateway treated the runtime assistant-error callback as if it guaranteed a Control UI-visible `chat.history` assistant turn.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Previous coverage asserted live error broadcast behavior but did not prove refreshed `chat.history` retained an assistant error after a started real runtime failure.
- Contributing context (if known): Runtime/session guard error stubs can be internal transcript-only markers; Gateway still needs to durably mirror returned terminal agent errors into the Control UI history path.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`, and the temporary real Gateway/WebSocket proof harness.
- Scenario the test should lock in: A started run returns an agent error; Gateway appends one assistant error transcript entry, keeps the live error terminal event, survives reconnect/history refresh, and does not duplicate the assistant error.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The source tests cover the exact Gateway and runner seams, while the real proof exercises server startup, WebSocket RPC, provider transport failure, `agent.wait`, and refreshed `chat.history` in one flow.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing broadcast tests covered visible error delivery but not durable assistant transcript history.
- If no new test is added, why not: New regression assertions were added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Control UI users should now see the same failed assistant turn after refresh that they saw live when a started agent run fails before normal assistant output.

## Diagram

```text
real provider/runtime failure
  -> runner returns final failure payload marked isError
  -> Gateway returnedAgentErrorPayloads branch
  -> append one sanitized assistant error transcript entry with <runId>:assistant-error
  -> broadcast existing chat error terminal event
  -> reconnect chat.history still includes one assistant error
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No.
- Data access scope changed? No new scope; the change writes a sanitized assistant error to the same session transcript path already used for Gateway assistant persistence.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Not applicable.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux disposable test checkout
- Runtime/container: OpenClaw repository scripts with installed dependencies
- Model/provider: OpenAI-compatible provider configured to an unreachable local base URL for deterministic transport failure; no real credentials used
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI-style Gateway WebSocket RPC; external channels skipped with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`
- Relevant config (redacted): Dummy redacted API key, local unreachable base URL, temporary state directory

### Steps

1. Start the real Gateway server from the patched checkout.
2. Connect a WebSocket client and send `chat.send` for a Control UI session.
3. Trigger a deterministic provider transport failure through the unreachable model base URL.
4. Wait for the run with `agent.wait`.
5. Read `chat.history` immediately.
6. Reconnect a fresh WebSocket client and read `chat.history` again.
7. Count assistant text messages and chat error events.

### Expected

- The run surfaces a live terminal error.
- Refreshed `chat.history` contains exactly one assistant error message for the failed run.
- The assistant error is not duplicated.

### Actual

- Before: refreshed `chat.history` had zero assistant messages for the failed run.
- After: refreshed `chat.history` had exactly one assistant error message and one chat error event.

## Evidence

- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

```text
After real proof result: ONE_ASSISTANT_ERROR_IN_REFRESHED_HISTORY
After source proof: Gateway focused 3/3, Gateway adjacent 107/107, session guard 35/35, Runner/Followup focused 8/8.
```

- Verified scenarios: Returned agent-run error payload detection, runner final failure `isError` marking, Gateway assistant-error transcript append, runtime internal error-stub callback interaction, idempotency key behavior, real provider transport failure, immediate history, refreshed history after reconnect, and existing chat error event behavior.
- Edge cases checked: Runtime internal error stub does not suppress the Control UI-visible returned error transcript; Gateway writes one `<runId>:assistant-error` entry for the returned terminal error; no dependency or lockfile files changed.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: A runtime-internal error stub could suppress the Control UI-visible returned error transcript.
  - Mitigation: Returned terminal agent errors now force the Gateway assistant-error transcript append while retaining the existing idempotency key.
- Risk: Gateway could duplicate assistant errors.
  - Mitigation: The transcript append uses a stable `<runId>:assistant-error` idempotency key and the source tests cover the one-message behavior.
- Risk: Error text could bypass display sanitization.
  - Mitigation: The Gateway fallback still uses `sanitizeAssistantDisplayText` before transcript append.

target.title: fix: split image setup and request timeout semantics
target.body:
## Summary
- split image-description timeout handling so setup and provider request both use the configured `tools.media.image.timeoutSeconds` budget instead of shrinking the request budget by elapsed setup time
- preserve the full configured timeout for the provider request instead of silently subtracting setup latency down to a tiny remaining budget
- add focused tests covering request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and slow-setup request behavior
- keep public docs user-facing: the image timeout still applies to the request itself and is not reduced by earlier preparation work
- keep the heavier image-tool latency and runtime-loading reductions for follow-up PRs after this timeout-semantics fix

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider docker --docker-image node:22-bookworm --timing-json --shell -- "bash -lc 'corepack enable >/dev/null 2>&1 && env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 corepack pnpm test src/media-understanding/image.test.ts'"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: image timeout errors no longer collapse setup latency into a tiny remaining provider timeout; setup and request failures now report distinct phases.
Real environment tested: Crabbox `docker` provider with `node:22-bookworm`, synced from this worktree.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: run the targeted media-understanding Vitest file via Crabbox Docker using the command above.
Evidence after fix: `src/media-understanding/image.test.ts` passes with 25 tests; assertions cover request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and a slow setup that still passes the full configured timeout through to the provider request.
Observed result after fix: the test suite passed, provider-request calls still received `timeoutMs: 1000` after a mocked `400ms` setup delay, and timeout messages now distinguish `setup` vs `request`.
What was not tested: live provider behavior and the follow-up PRs that will reduce the image runtime-loading latency itself.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`
